mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are uncorrelated with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero mean forward return over 60 days in backtests, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, a decline in the index signals supply-side weakness that reduces corporate revenue forecasts, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production declines, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside contraction phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes prohibitive for marginal equities, causing immediate downward pressure on the S&P 500 over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in over 60% of observations when the funds rate is above 4.5%, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns over 60 days, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, accelerating real GDP growth signals improving corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction in subsequent quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate sell-offs in cyclical equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 records positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases following a 0.5% industrial production drop, or the threshold lacks predictive significance.
